prompt:
Build hooks_runtime.ts — a miniature React-style hooks runtime so the team can teach (and property-test) the call-order rules without pulling in React. No React import, no JSX anywhere; plain TypeScript that runs under node's type stripping (type annotations only — no enums/namespaces/parameter properties — and keep explicit ./file.ts specifiers).

Exports and contract:

- createRoot(component) where component is (props) => output (any return type). Returns { render(props) }. Each root owns its own hook slots; two roots of the same component function are fully independent. render(props) invokes the component and returns the LAST invocation's return value (see the rerender loop).

- Hooks are identified by call order (slot 0, 1, 2, ... per invocation). Calling any hook while no render is in progress throws Error("hooks may only be called while a component is rendering"). Calling render while any render is already in progress (same root or another) throws Error("render is not reentrant"). A render that throws for any reason must release that lock — a later render of another root works fine.

- useState(initial): returns [value, setter]. If initial is a function it is a lazy initializer, called exactly once when the slot is created (never on re-renders). The setter accepts either a plain value or an updater function (prev => next) and QUEUES it on the slot; the setter's identity is stable across renders. A slot's queue drains at the moment its hook is called during an invocation: FIFO, a plain value replaces state, an updater is called with the latest pending state.

- useReducer(reducer, initial): returns [state, dispatch]. initial is used as-is — even when it is a function, it is NOT invoked. dispatch(action) queues the action (stable identity); the queue drains FIFO at the hook call, state = reducer(state, action) per action.

- Rerender loop: after the component returns, if any slot queue is non-empty (e.g. a setter/dispatch ran during render), re-invoke the component with the same props. Settled output is returned. Hard bound: if after the 25th invocation updates are still pending, throw Error("too many re-renders (limit 25)") — the 26th invocation never runs.

- Call-order enforcement: when a hook call hits an existing slot created by a different hook, throw Error(`hook order changed at slot ${i}: expected ${prev}, got ${next}`) with the hook names spelled useState/useReducer/useMemo (e.g. "hook order changed at slot 0: expected useState, got useReducer"). After each completed invocation, compare the number of hooks called against the previous completed invocation and throw Error(`hook count changed: expected ${m}, got ${n}`) when it differs (both fewer and more).

- useMemo(factory, deps): deps is an array. Recompute (call factory) on the first call, when the deps length changes, or when any element fails Object.is against the previous deps; otherwise return the cached value (same reference). Note Object.is: NaN equals NaN.

Acceptance: `node --test test_hooks_runtime.ts` fully green. The test file is read-only and pins everything above, including the exact error message strings and invocation counts.
notes: react-internals build seed: NO vendored React, no JSX — plain .ts under node type stripping, node --test. hooks_runtime.ts is absent in the baseline (module-not-found is the pre-fix failure). Pinned semantics chosen to mirror React where cheap (lazy useState initializer, stable setter/dispatch identity, FIFO update queue with updater-fn threading, Object.is deps, render-phase-update rerender loop with a 25 bound, hook count/order errors) but with exact deterministic error strings so the suite can assert them.
